title: The Future of Education
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Rapid (0) technological change is reshaping education systems around the world. Online learning platforms and digital tools are making (17) knowledge more accessible than ever before, allowing students to study at their own pace regardless of location. (18) Interactive software can adapt to the needs of individual learners, providing a more (19) personalised experience than traditional classroom teaching.||However, the (20) widespread adoption of technology in education also raises concerns. Many teachers argue that face-to-face (21) interaction is essential for developing social skills and emotional intelligence. There are also worries about the (22) increasing amount of time students spend on screens. Experts agree that the most (23) effective approach is to combine digital tools with (24) traditional teaching methods, creating a balanced and stimulating learning environment.

example.text: Rapid (0) TECHNOLOGICAL change is reshaping education systems around the world. (TECHNOLOGY)

questions.explanation: The noun 'knowledge' is formed from 'know' by adding the suffix '-ledge'.
The adjective 'interactive' is formed from 'interact' by adding the suffix '-ive'.
The adjective 'personalised' is formed from 'personal' by adding the suffix '-ised'.
The adjective 'widespread' is a compound formed from 'wide' and 'spread'.
The noun 'interaction' is formed from 'interact' by adding the suffix '-ion'.
The adjective/gerund 'increasing' is formed from 'increase' by adding the suffix '-ing'.
The adjective 'effective' is formed from 'effect' by adding the suffix '-ive'.
The adjective 'traditional' is formed from 'tradition' by adding the suffix '-al'.
